comment: Single source of truth for ACCEPTED / known divergences from C Graphviz. Joined at report-time into PARITY.md (dashboard.ts) and the rules gate (rules-gate.ts) via accepted.ts — parity.json itself stays pure (verdict only). An accepted delta is a deliberate, documented, won't-fix difference; everything non-conformant NOT listed here is an unaccepted 'tracked' gap we intend to close. Adding/removing an entry must be paired with the prose in docs/known-divergences.md (the accepted-divergences.test.ts guard enforces that per-id parity entries are still non-conformant, so the list cannot rot silently). match: {id} exact | {idPattern} glob | {engineIn} engine list. scope: parity (dashboard) | rules (rules-gate) | both.

entries.bound: fine node coordinates; topology + ranks preserved, positions within a small epsilon
entries.reason: iterative force-model layouts depend on FMA / Math.pow rounding that is not bit-reproducible across JS engines and CPUs (same boundary as the Arm-pow port). Matches zero graphs in the all-dot corpus today; kept for completeness.

entries.bound: maxΔ~10pt on one flat-edge arc (376->76); bbox/nodes/labels conformant
entries.reason: symmetric-channel bezier-split tie broken by ~1e-14 noise; Apple macOS libm hypot is position-dependent (mirrors congruent arcs) and matches no portable hypot, so the port keeps a consistent translation-equivariant tie-break.

entries.bound: maxΔ 20 (one rank-row) on one hub in-edge knot (Node23730->Node23729); every node + all upstream box/poly/taut-path byte-identical to C
entries.reason: same symmetric-channel bezier-split tie as 2368: findMaxDev picks between two exactly mirror-symmetric interior knots whose deviations differ by ~1 ULP; Apple libm hypot is position-dependent, so the port keeps its translation-equivariant tolerant tie-break. Reverting to C's strict tie-break is a survey net-wash and regresses oracle-pinned #241_0/cnt=3 tests. See .agent-notes/hub-fanin-longedge-diagnosis.md.

entries.bound: maxΔ 20 (one rank-row) on one hub in-edge knot; identical source to graphs-b100, same symmetric-channel tie-break
entries.reason: identical dot source to graphs-b100 (diff = 0), same findMaxDev ~1-ULP hypot tie on a mirror-symmetric channel; irreducible portability constraint, same as 2368.

entries.bound: only the top-level svg width/height differ; every node ellipse/coord, translate, polygon, and font-size is byte-identical
entries.reason: fuzzer input (fontsize="991836031967s8") balloons the drawing to ~2.75e11pt. C's job->width/height are unsigned int (gvcjob.h:327-328): the ROUND result (emit.c:1249-1250) wraps mod 2^32 and the SVG emits it via signed %d (gvrender_core_svg.c:258-259), so C prints height="-425618343". The port keeps the mathematically-consistent unwrapped value; replicating C's 32-bit overflow is not a layout behavior worth porting. Degenerate input; revisit if upstream fixes the overflow.

entries.bound: maxΔ 49 numeric only; identical element tree (both sides lose edge 3->16)
entries.reason: oracle lays out from an acknowledged-broken init_rank recovery state (aux-graph cycle; upstream xfail #2796, draft MR !4849, cf. #2471). The verify-oracle-bug-family mission fixed the port's stale-cluster-window defect (rec_reset_vlists, flat.c:333) making the x-aux inputs line-identical to C; the port now follows C through the recovery (same lost edge 3->16, 212/212). Residual numeric deltas are recovery-state NS behavior, deliberately not chased. Re-measure when upstream fixes #2796.

entries.bound: whole-layout numeric (maxΔ ~9063); element tree identical incl. the same 6 lost edges
entries.reason: oracle in the same acknowledged-broken init_rank recovery family (upstream xfail #2471, worst instance). Ranking + x-aux constraint inputs verified line-identical (253 rank2 calls) after the family fixes; both sides lose the identical 6 edges via C's triangulation debris. Residual numeric deltas are NS behavior from the infeasible/cyclic recovery state — not chased. Re-measure when upstream fixes #2471.

entries.bound: maxΔ 503 on pathplan-fallback spline debris; 11/11 edges on both sides; nodes/clusters conformant
entries.reason: acknowledged-broken pathplan input (upstream xfail #1435): both sides hit ear-clip triangulation dead ends and recover through warn-and-continue + straight-line fallbacks whose debris differs. All 16 NS constraint dumps line-identical; the port's fatal-triangulation misport was fixed (shortest.c:333 parity) so no edges are lost on either side. Re-measure when upstream fixes #1435.

entries.bound: maxΔ 0; element-tree difference only (the oracle's lost struct1->struct3)
entries.reason: the dev-build headless oracle loses struct1:f2->struct3:here to a Pshortestpath failure (ancient record-routing loss family #102/#242/#274/#1323); the port routes both edges from line-identical NS inputs and identical node geometry (maxΔ 0). Stable graphviz 15.0.0 also renders it — oracle-side regression, not a port gap.

node geometry 0.00; the dev-build headless oracle (15.1.0~dev) drops struct1:f2->struct3:here via a Pshortestpath regression. Stable graphviz 15.0.0 AND the port both render it (edge1 conformant to graphviz.org). Oracle-build regression, not a port bug.
node widths match headless exactly (rx 34.64); residual 1pt is node2's x under a :sw compass-port edge — a pre-existing x-NS/compass-port gap the LUT widths masked, not measurement.

entries.bound: maxDelta 0; exactly one structural diff (one <title> byte: raw 0x80 vs UTF-8 U+0080); all element counts and coordinates conformant
entries.reason: input contains a naked UTF-8 trail byte in a node name; C passes it through raw (utils.c:1200-1207 'valid characters representing themselves') yielding invalid-UTF-8 SVG output. The port's JS-string I/O cannot represent a raw invalid byte in renderSvg's return value; the latin1 fallback decode (0x80 -> U+0080) matches C's own 'treated as Latin-1' recovery semantics. Irreducible at the representation layer.

entries.bound: maxDelta 465; top-level childCount 39 vs 22 (oracle recovery discards content)
entries.reason: fuzzer input; upstream test_1581 asserts only no memory-safety violation (issue #1581). C hits 'Error: install_in_rank' and its recovery drops ranks; the port reaches the identical rankset deletions (warning parity: Act_23/Act_24 from G) without the internal inconsistency and lays out the surviving graph. Oracle output is crash-recovery debris with no upstream-defined semantics.

entries.bound: maxΔ ~2.4pt on one flat-edge arc; bbox/nodes/labels conformant
entries.reason: same symmetric-channel bezier-split hypot tie as 2368/241_0: findMaxDev picks between mirror-symmetric interior knots differing by ~1 ULP; Apple libm hypot is position-dependent, so the port keeps its translation-equivariant tie-break. Adopting C's strict tie-break regresses the 241_0/cnt=3 oracle cases (net wash). Same primitive as 2368.

entries.bound: maxΔ ~8pt; element tree identical, same init_rank/pathplan recovery family as 2471
entries.reason: oracle in the same acknowledged-broken init_rank recovery family as 2471/1435/2796 (upstream xfail). The port reaches the identical rankset-deletion/recovery decisions; residual numeric deltas are NS behavior from the infeasible recovery state, not chased. Re-measure when upstream fixes the family.

entries.bound: maxΔ ~1pt on one edge (n012->n011); every node/label/bbox conformant
entries.reason: maximal_bbox head-corridor box wall lands at internal x=90 in C vs x=89 in the port — a round()/box-wall rounding-boundary artifact of ~1e-14 upstream float noise tipping a .5 boundary. The port's maximal_bbox formula mirrors C exactly; round() is a shared primitive every routed edge flows through, so changing it is corpus-wide regression risk for 1px on 2 edges. Full diagnosis: .agent-notes/honda-samehead-shared-port.md.

entries.bound: maxΔ 67.65 on the labeled 2-cycle back-edge label-vnode slit corridor; nodes/bbox conformant
entries.reason: same findMaxDev symmetric-tie family as 2368/241_1/b100/b104: the two candidate split corners of the label-vnode slit corridor tie to within 5.7e-13 of each other; Apple libm hypot (route.c:139) flips the winner per absolute coordinate (exact translates of the same corridor resolve differently in one process), so the port keeps its translation-equivariant keeps-first tie-break. Ruled out: route/box divergence (byte-identical corridors across 3 exact translates), 2-cycle double-count recurrence (nodes byte-identical), routing order (identical both sides).

entries.bound: maxΔ ≤99.55 (two findMaxDev ties, same corridor family as 2413_1) once the T11 swapBezier-reverse fix lands; until then parity.json reports maxΔ 1922.26, dominated by that separate, already-diagnosed-and-fixed swapBezier over-allocated-reverse defect (not part of this acceptance)
entries.reason: same findMaxDev symmetric-tie family as 2368/241_1/2413_1: two independent label-vnode slit-corridor splits tie to within 5.7e-13; Apple libm hypot flips the winner per absolute coordinate, so the port keeps its translation-equivariant keeps-first tie-break. The much larger current maxΔ (1922.26) is a distinct, unrelated mechanism — swapBezier's full-array reverse rotating 3 stale (0,0) slots to the front on a size-shrunk reversed back edge (same bug as T1/hub-fanin) — tracked as a fix (T11), not accepted; once merged this entry's residual is the A3 tie only.

entries.bound: maxΔ 43.54 on a single intra-cluster labeled edge (Se3ffa656...->Se3ffa61c...); all 23 diffs live in that one <g>
entries.reason: same findMaxDev symmetric-tie family as 2368/241_1/2413_1/2413_2: interior deviations tie at 77.56953556793451 vs ...54 (~3e-14 abs, ~4e-16 rel) — exact double-noise tie. C's raw `>` comparison via Apple libm hypot keeps-second; the port's tolerant tie-break keeps-first; the mirrored split cascades to the arrowhead and decorate connector (closest-point-on-spline). Ruled out (each against live C-instrumented rebuilds): box construction/maximal_bbox/rank_box, adjust/complete regular path widening, beginPath/endPath dispatch, buildPolyPoints/flip, shortestPath funnel, evs tangents, routeSplinesInternal inputs (all byte-identical).

entries.bound: maxΔ 16.8 on two edges (g[9263] r6837mid--r9687mid, g[23859] r38mid--r8699mid); the port emits the exact control-point-sequence mirror of the oracle on both; all ~36,900 other elements byte-identical
entries.reason: joins the existing findMaxDev/hypot symmetric-tie family (A3, cf. 2368): both divergent edges emit the exact reversal of the oracle's control-point sequence — shared knot x identical, knot y flipped by an identical Δ 16.8 on both unrelated edges (top/bottom split fractions swapped) — the same irreducible hypot-ULP tie-break as 2368. Origin confidence is qualified MEDIUM: 2371 packs ~199 components, decoupling pathplan-local coordinates from page coordinates, so the tie could not be correlated live to route.ts:209 across three instrumentation strategies; not fully excluded that the tie resolves in straight-mode segmentation or post-clip recover_slack instead of literally at that site. Full diagnosis: plans/residual-cleanup/analysis/2371-mirror.md.

entries.bound: maxΔ 42.09 on 3 of 21,216 edges (edge2575 g[4639], edge3905 g[7777], edge15467 g[30201]; all record-port :c->:nb_part smode long-edge routes)
entries.reason: final smode leg tangent to the barrier wall (endpoint on wall, tangent parallel); splinefits a-halving exit flips on solve3 disc sign, decided by clang/arm64 -ffp-contract FMA rounding (~9e-13) in points2coeff/solve3 under catastrophic cancellation; port == strict-IEEE C (verified bit-exact via -ffp-contract=off harness, all 3 edges); flips both directions; maxΔ 42.09 on 3 of 21,216 edges. Full diagnosis: plans/residual-cleanup/analysis/2646-fp-contract.md.

entries.bound: 423 diffs / maxΔ 585 on 24 edge paths + 22 arrowheads
entries.reason: 423 diffs / maxΔ585 on 24 edge paths + 22 arrowheads. The ortho pipeline is byte-conformant to C given identical inputs (proven by full input injection: 378/378 routes byte-identical). The divergence is 1-2 ULP in node sizes from clang arm64 -ffp-contract=on FMA fusion in poly_init's vertex-extent loop (shapes.c; port is strict-IEEE, cannot emit fma), amplified by ortho's faithful per-relax int-truncation (sgraph.c:165) into an equal-cost corridor tie flip on 4 edges. Proven by a controlled -ffp-contract on/off experiment: on → 310.29250168188713 (=C), off → 310.29250168188707 (=port). Port == strict-IEEE C; irreducible without emulating clang's fma contraction. Full diagnosis: plans/ortho-2620-residual/analysis/2620-ortho-route.md.
